notes: PRIMARY WORK — resolved BOTH high-severity coverage-audit gaps (08:24 run). (1) FIFA World Cup later rounds were NOT emitted by the static ESPN pipeline: added 4 ai-research events — semifinal 1 (Tue 14 July, Dallas/AT&T Stadium, TV 2), semifinal 2 (Wed 15 July, Atlanta/Mercedes-Benz, TV 2 — the Norway/England winner feeds here), bronze final (Sat 18 July, Miami/Hard Rock, NRK) and the FINAL (Sun 19 July, MetLife, NRK). Dates/venues/times cross-confirmed by ESPN + Wikipedia via a fan-out scout and an independent fresh-context fact-checker; Norwegian rights (TV 2 semis / NRK bronze+final) confirmed by VG + oddspodden. (2) F1 Belgian GP (Sun 19 July) + Hungarian GP (Sun 26 July), both 13:00 UTC / 15:00 CEST, confirmed against formula1.com + RacingNews365 — and the static board already shows them correctly Sunday-dated (the audit's Friday-dating had cleared), so no F1 event was needed; only the tracked reason updated.

DUPLICATE CLEANUP: removed the ai-research 'Norge – England' (Norway QF) because ESPN has now resolved its bracket placeholder into a static event 'England at Norway' (homeTeam Norway, awayTeam England, norwegian:true, TV 2) on the same 11 July 21:00 UTC slot. Static pipeline covers the match durably; removing my duplicate avoids a double-render on the flagship Norwegian match. Only my own ai-research event was removed — the 'never remove static-pipeline events' rule is respected, and build-events does not resurrect a deleted ai-research entry.

NEW: Diamond League Monaco (Herculis EBS), Fri 10 July, Stade Louis-II — Norwegian Sigrid Borge in the javelin (start 17:30 CEST / 15:30 UTC; event time set to the javelin so the Norwegian angle is the useful moment). On NRK (Wanda DL rights 2025–2029). Confidence medium (Borge's entry from the official Monaco programme, single source). Fact-checker amended the time from the meet-level 18:00 UTC to the javelin's 15:30 UTC.

DELIBERATELY NOT ADDED: Jakob Ingebrigtsen comeback — his agent named Monaco / Budapest / London as possible July returns but 'does not want to commit yet' (speculative, unverifiable). NM friidrett Trondheim 23–25 July is real but just outside the 2-week window; noted in tracked for the next run.

TdF: wildfire affected stage 3 (6 July, ran without spectators on the last ~40 km, no time/route change); stage 4 (7 July) on Code Red heat alert (~41°C) with the UCI protocol on standby but NO change confirmed as of 6 July afternoon — verify must recheck stage 4 the morning of 7 July.

DURABILITY CHECK (the recurring streaming-revert risk): the WC rights map (norwegian-rights.js) returns a tentative 'NRK / TV 2' for any World Cup fixture, but build-events.js preserves a previously-CONFIRMED (non-tentative) channel over a tentative re-guess (prevConfirmedStreaming). My TV 2 / NRK 1 values are non-tentative, so they survive the hourly rebuild; ai-research events are also always kept by the relevance filter. No norwegian-rights.js edit needed this run.

Dismissed noise: coverage-gaps.json had one low-severity chess file-empty anomaly (off-season static fetcher; Sjakk-NM is ai-research and on the board) and one non-imminent PL entity gap (Glasner → Nottingham Forest, a coaching-news headline, not an event). scout-log: 21x 'quiet' over the last day. validate-events.js: 56 events, 0 errors.
